title: What Makes HP Large Format Media Different?
description:
What does it take to earn the HP logo? Months of testing. Sometimes years. HP large format media goes through rigorous quality, permanence, and sustainability testing before it ever ships. 

Every product in the portfolio is built to resist light, air, humidity, fading, thermal degradation, and shelf-life issues. HP ink, printers, and media are engineered to work together as a complete system, eliminating compatibility guesswork and delivering consistent results. 

The portfolio also includes FSC, recycled, GREENGUARD Gold certified, OEKO-tex, and REACH compliant options for environmentally conscious shops.

title: Berger Textiles | Spec the Right Fabric for Every Application
description:
If your soft signage is coming off the printer with inconsistent color, loose SEG frames, or drape that doesn't hang right, the problem probably isn't your ink or your printer. It's the fabric.

Berger Textiles brings over 175 years of textile expertise and dedicated in-house engineers to every product in their line. These aren't fabrics that just happen to work for printing. They're materials developed specifically for soft signage performance, tested rigorously and continuously to deliver consistent results roll after roll. From lab values and white point to stretch, shrinkage, and drape, every characteristic that affects your output is monitored and controlled.

Products featured in this video:
Phantom Blackback - High white point fabric designed for reverse image applications where crispness and contrast are critical

Lumina - Industry-leading woven backlit fabric with superior light diffusion and a premium feel, ideal for backlit signage displays

Aurora - Premium knit backlit fabric with ultra-fine yarns, exceptional ink density, and one of the softest finishes available, best for applications where image quality and surface feel are both priorities

Berger textiles are also compatible across UV, UV gel, latex, solvent, and dye sublimation workflows, so a single SKU can serve multiple production setups in your shop. Our Berger sales team at Brand Management Group is here to help you spec the right fabric for every application with confidence.

description:

In this video, we showcase Berger Gotham Black Back fabric paired with the Fotoba XLD170 cutter, highlighting how this combination helps print shops eliminate one of their biggest bottlenecks—manual cutting.

Gotham Black Back is engineered for high opacity and consistent print performance, making it ideal for display graphics where light control is critical. When combined with the XLD 170’s high-speed cross-cutting system, print providers can process long runs quickly and with precision.

This setup enables shops to deliver faster, reduce labor strain, and take on larger jobs with confidence.

Applications include:
• Black back display graphics
• Trade show and event signage
• Retail graphics
• Soft signage
• Wall and booth graphics
• High-volume textile workflows

description:

In this video, we showcase Berger Samba fabric running on the Fotoba XLA170, demonstrating how the right textile can unlock the full potential of automated cutting. Samba is known for its versatility, stability, and compatibility across multiple print technologies, making it ideal for workflows that demand precision.

The XLA170’s optical barcode system automatically reads each job and positions cuts without operator input, allowing print shops to scale production while reducing errors. Samba’s consistent behavior ensures clean cuts and reliable results across every run.

Applications include:
• Wallpaper and wallcoverings
• SEG fabric graphics
• Backlit textile displays
• Retail graphics
• Event and trade show signage
• High-volume textile production

This combination helps print providers print more, cut faster, and deliver on time.

title: When Should You Laminate Digital Prints?
description: Why should you laminate digital prints? Lamination extends the life of a print. It protects it from abrasion, chemicals, UV exposure, dirt, moisture, graffiti, and tearing. Clear coating or “liquid laminate” is a cost-effective, easy-to-apply lamination and is especially ideal for protecting textured surfaces. Marabu's solvent- and water-based coatings are premium-grade, high-performance liquid laminates that are less expensive than films.

title: Smile, 5 Fun Foto Facts about the Invention of the Camera
description:
June 29 is a day that commemorates a device that has changed the world: The Camera.

George Eastman, the founder of Kodak,is known as “The Father of Photography,”because he brought the camera to the masses.

Kodak Wide-Format Media celebrates photography every day of the year, but especially on this day we thought it would be fun to share these five fun foto facts.

Fun Foto Fact

In 1900, the Kodak Brownie was released; the first camera affordable to the common man, it cost $1 and a roll of film cost 15¢.

The first photo ever taken of the earth was captured by Lunar Orbiter I with a Kodak camera system.

The word camera comes from camera obscura, which means "dark chamber." The first cameras used a pinhole and a dark room.

In 1888, George Eastman trademarked the name “Kodak”, a meaningless word that would soon be synonymous with photography and become one of the most recognizable brands in the world. 

Steve Sassen, an engineer at Kodak who worked in R&D invented the digital camera.

title: Print’s New Place in the Retail Rebound

A few of the new retail signage trends include:

Signage that adds to shopping as an experience 
Signage that can help ease and facilitate shopping such as navigational and instructional signs for self-checkout, digital wallets, and queue lines
Bold endcap displays that can make it easy to “grab-and-go”
Pop-up displays can make an in-store connection with online shoppers who are there for pickup
QR codes on signs to provide additional and immediate product and promotional information that may not be available to online shoppers
Point-of-purchase displays for essentials reducing the time spent in-store
Outdoor signage to draw shoppers in

Print and technology together will continue to connect the online and offline shopping experience and create opportunities for Print Service Providers. HP offers media to create a multitude of applications for both aqueous and latex ink technologies to help retailers meet these new challenges.

title: Artist turns love of photography into a thriving printing business
description:
Rob Penner knows something about fine art printing. For the past 35 years, Penner has been involved in all facets of photography, printing, and fine art, from production to post-production. He started his career as a fashion photographer in New York in the heady days of the ‘70s; photographing the likes of Diana Ross and Bianca Jagger and frequenting Andy Warhol’s Factory and Studio 54. He attended Pratt Institute and started working in advertising doing graphic production where he learned all aspects of printing from traditional press to digital printing. He opened Beacon Fine Art Printing, specializing in digital archival printing, custom art reproduction, edition printing for artists, collectors and galleries while still doing his own personal photography.

“It’s helpful having experience on both sides of the coin, seeing things as a visual artist, then being able to turn them into a graphic production platform — taking someone’s idea and turning it into a tangible piece of art,” Penner explains.

He likes KODAK PROFESSIONAL Inkjet Photo Paper, Lustre for his customers who frame their own work as it produces vibrant colors ensuring that colors captured in-camera or on a smartphone reproduce more accurately. KODAK PROFESSIONAL Inkjet Photo Paper, Lustre has been updated to take advantage of the wider color gamut and improved ink technology of the latest generation of wide format printers from Canon, Epson and HP. title: COVID-19 Impact on Digital Decor
description:
COVID-19 has presented new challenges for designers and architects in creating safe workplaces, medical facilities, educational institutions and public spaces in general. However, the new focus on healthy environments and infection control has also brought about positive inspirations. 

A few of the future design changes that will affect digital decor include:

1.     Easy to clean materials:
We'll see the use of more smooth surface products that can be cleaned and disinfected easily. People will be looking for products that meet health and safety requirements but at the same time, not look too institutional. 
·         Vescom Pearl Smooth 20 oz Non Woven wallcovering fits the bill by providing a smooth un-embossed satin surface that prints beautiful images with a Type II rating that stands up to cleaning with strong chemicals.
 
2.     Designing to the WELL Building Standard:
No longer a bonus, it will become standard to design to WELL Building criteria which focus on low chemical emitting products and antimicrobial surfaces. 
·         Vescom Digital Wallcoverings are phthalate-free and meet EPA requirements for low VOC emitting. For additional safety assurance, Vescom wallcovering can be coated with the antimicrobial coating—Mara®Ban CS.
 
3.      Addition of Separation Walls: 
Interior spaces will see the addition of walls for separation in home, retail and corporate environments. Offices will move away from the “open” layout and return to the partitions of the ‘80s to promote infection control and providing an additional need for wallcovering.
 
4.     Supply Chain Issues: 
The current climate has seen supply chain problems due to the negative impact on the availability of raw materials from overseas making USA products and shipping a plus. 
·         Vescom Digital Wallcovering is fully stocked and available with warehouses that ship from the U.S.
 
The digital décor industry, like every other industry, will see changes but will endure, adapt and continue to thrive—even in the aftermath of this crisis.
 
To find out more information or to order, contact your Vescom Specialist at 888-893-4668 and visit us at http://www.vescomdecor.com.

title: 5 tips to help PSPs enter the Digital Décor Market
description:
The digital decor market was valued at $18.9 billion in 2018* and was forecast to grow 5% annually until 2023, providing a huge market opportunity for the print industry. In the current COVID-19 climate, PSPs need to look for alternative opportunities and revenue streams. Here are a few tips to help you or your customers take steps to enter this new market. 

1. Create an on-demand website
Work with a web designer to create a website that allows people to create, upload and print personalized content on-demand.

2. Automate the design and print process with our HP Wallart Suite
This easy and innovative web-to-print software allows printers to manage everything from design, to order information in real-time online. The software comes with four free decoration web apps (wallcovering, canvas, wall décals and posters) and is free to HP Latex printer owners. All you need is your serial number.

3. Offer digital decor printing to your existing customers first, before branching into new markets
If your bread and butter niche is architects and engineers, offer wall murals, decals, custom canvas art and show how they can enhance their office space and promote their product or service through digital decor.

4. Partner with an Interior Designer
Interior Design firms are creating more personalized environments and looking to print everything from curtains, pillows, wallcovering and more.

5. Create a portfolio of examples
For samples and other marketing collateral, you can download assets from our HP Support Hub or we can help customize them with your logo and company contact information.

title: Master Installer Chooses Vescom Wallpaper for Student Housing at UCSD
description:
The University of California, San Diego recently updated three student housing buildings with 35 new digitally printed wall murals. The dormitories at the La Jolla campus were being considered as an “alternate care setting” for hospital patients during the pandemic, but ultimately were not needed. Master Installer, Greg McAllister, owner of All American Wallpapering, a member of WIA with 40 years of experience, was looking for a non-woven, Type II compliant wallcovering for this important project when he found Vescom Polster 20 oz Non Woven.
 
“I highly recommend Vescom wallcovering,” says Greg, “There are very few non-woven products on the market so I was happy to find Vescom Polster. I used the 54-inch wide size and pasted the wall, not the paper—it went up seamlessly and was installed in far less time! I needed a durable paper for this heavily trafficked space and Vescom wallcovering is Type II compliant, so I was assured of its durability. This material is also safe for sensitive environments such as schools, hospitals and public buildings.”
 
Non-woven wallpaper is fast becoming designers, installers, consumers and printer’s preferred substrate. Really the only substrate suitable for digital printing, it is known for its sturdiness, easy application and seamless results. Vescom wallcovering is also dry strippable, meaning no steaming and scraping to remove. title: Printed Posters Blanket a Dutch City with Positivity to Counter COVID-19
description:
Residents of Breda, a city in the Netherlands hard hit by the coronavirus, can now look out their windows to see colorful, uplifting posters spread across the city as a reaction to the pandemic. The posters are a result of the Stay Safe, Stay Sane (https://stay-sane-stay-safe.com/) project created by Lennarts & De Brujin (http://www.lennartsendebruijn.com/) design agency and Overdeshreef lettering agency to support and praise the city’s healthcare and essential workers. The posters were also printed and hung in hospitals and healthcare facilities. 

Thousands of designers from all over the world have contributed creative, cutting-edge posters that are offered as free downloads to be printed and installed in windows and walls worldwide to encourage efforts to combat this virus. title: Vescom Presents - The Transformation of Saint-François de Paule School
description:
Saint-François de Paule School in Fréjus, France, needed graphics to cover their windows for security purposes but the project soon evolved into an interior design re-do of the entire school. L’agence Riccobono of France designed unique graphics for the classrooms that represent three climate zones and their corresponding plant and animal life—tropical, temperate and polar. The goal was to make students aware of the diversity and richness of life on our planet and the importance of preserving it.  The designers created four characters, a girl, boy, dog and parrot who travel throughout the scenes and are integrated into teaching materials to reinforce learning. The project was so impressive it was nominated for the National Icona d'Or competition for the best design project of a school.

title: Wildlife photographer champions the beauty of Yellowstone with a mission to protect
description:
Armed with his large-format camera, Wim van Passel, wildlife photographer for the World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF), has gone on yet another wilderness adventure creating poignant photographs of the natural beauty and magnificence of our own Yellowstone National Park. The country’s first national park, sits atop a “sleeping giant”—the world’s largest supervolcano, reminding us how violent and powerful Mother Earth can be. The park is home to a variety of wildlife, from bears to bison, and geological stunners such as hot springs and geysers.
 
 “The majesty and natural power of Yellowstone make me contemplate the creation of this planet and what’s going on “inside” our world,” says van Passel. “Nature continues to amaze and we have the privilege to look on and wonder….”
 
Van Passel donates his images to the WWF and other causes to promote conservation efforts, relying on support from company sponsorships. HP is proud to be a sponsor of this amazing artist and his quest to save our wild spaces with the thought that they may someday save us.
  
Van Passel prints on HP Premium Instant-dry Gloss Photo Paper, which dries instantly, produces exceptional image quality on long-lasting prints that meet environmental objectives with an FSC®-certification. 
 
“I would recommend this photo paper to anyone,” says van Passel, “After all these years of printing photography for over 125 exhibitions, there has never been a question about the quality of the print.” 
  
Offer the professional’s choice: HP Premium Instant-dry Gloss Photo Paper and learn about other HP Photo papers. Check out van Passel’s work on his website at http://www.tijdlozemomenten.nl. title: Profitable Printing Trends for 2020
description:
2020 promises to be an exciting time for the printing industry.  Experts predict a renaissance for the small printer due to innovations in workflow and technology that drive growth and productivity. Despite a move to paperless offices, print remains a critical part of business with an increased emphasis on security and a cloud-based service approach.

A few of 2020’s future growth areas in the large format print industry:

1) Increased Personalization: New software programs are making it easier to use variable data to personalize each printed piece with unique information from a spreadsheet or database.

2) Sustainable Printing is Here to Stay: Consumers are demanding sustainable practices and printers are realizing they can go green and still be profitable.

3) Printing On-the-Go: Technological advancements make it possible for printing companies to offer their services in a variety of ways, as well as expand their product portfolio, including printing-on-the-go, which allows customers to order prints from their smartphones or laptops. 

4) Greater Demand for Security: Customers print documents that contain sensitive information and an increase in sophisticated hacking creates a greater need for security.

5) The Rise of Digital Printing:  As the demand for customization and on-demand printing continues to grow, offset printing will become less valuable and printers will need to jump on the digital printing bandwagon. 

These trends of progressive technologies and creative methods offer opportunities to meet customer demands and cut costs, helping businesses thrive in today’s economy. HP Large Format Media provides you and your customers with products and support to help you profit from these trends.
